posts.title: Climate Momentum: Powering Change with Data
posts.author: Ava Torres
posts.summary: A mixed-media exploration of climate trends, civic action, and solutions that deliver measurable results.

cards.title: Why data matters
cards.body:
Sharing clear, accessible climate data turns concern into action.
When people see the progress we make together, they are more likely to stay engaged.

cards.title: Climate advocacy in motion
cards.caption: Volunteer organizers gather in a coastal community to build resilient climate campaigns.

cards.title: Community momentum
cards.description: volunteers engaged across 18 campaigns this year.

cards.title: Renewable Capacity Growth
cards.subtitle: Installed energy capacity in gigawatts across recent years.

cards.text: The strongest weapon against climate change is hope paired with action.
cards.author: Dr. Nia Rahman

cards.title: Then vs. Now
cards.leftText: Few cities tracked climate performance with local metrics.
cards.rightText: Coastal communities use data dashboards to monitor resilience outcomes.

cards.title: Featured analysis
cards.description: Explore a curated climate research brief with practical policy recommendations.

events.description: A regional coalition passed a zero-emissions transit commitment.
Restoration teams planted 22,000 mangroves along storm-prone shoreline.
Public workshops launched energy-saving programs for schools and community centers.

items.q: How can individuals make an impact?
items.a: Vote for climate-safe policy, support local advocacy groups, and reduce energy waste at home.
Stories build empathy and make statistics feel human, which helps turn audiences into advocates.

posts.title: Keeping Oceans Cool: Restoration and Resilience
posts.author: Noah Kim
posts.summary: Designing coastal resilience with nature-based solutions, science-backed campaigns, and communal stewardship.

cards.title: Nature-based resilience
cards.body:
Ocean restoration helps reduce heat and storm risk while supporting biodiversity.
This story highlights how coastal communities protect both people and habitat.

cards.title: Surface temperature decline
cards.subtitle: Ocean cooling trends after protection efforts begin.

cards.title: Engagement vs. risk reduction
cards.subtitle: The relationship between community participation and coastal protection outcomes.

cards.title: Resilience scorecard
cards.subtitle: Comparing ecosystem benefits across restoration themes.

items.q: What makes coastal restoration so effective?
items.a: Restoration harnesses natural systems to absorb heat, reduce erosion, and support fisheries in ways engineered structures cannot.

items.q: Can a single community make a difference?
items.a: Yes. Local action cascades into policy change, public awareness, and stronger regional resilience.
